stories.title: CPSC Recall — Cosyland Children's Tower Stools (Entrapment Hazard)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: 125,000+ Cosyland tower stools recalled.

Kids' torsos can fit through openings — entrapment + fall hazard. 8 injuries reported.

Models CS0003 + CS0092-4, sold Amazon ~$70.

Free repair kit: 866-677-3889 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Have a Cosyland tower stool? Stop using it now.

CPSC recall: 125,200 units, 8 injuries. Collapse + entrapment hazard.

Free repair kit from Cosyland — call 866-677-3889 or email customerservice@cosyland.com

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM MT

If you have a Cosyland tower stool at home, this is important.

The Consumer Product Safety Commission issued a recall on May 14 for Cosyland-brand children's tower stools — the kind designed to let kids reach counter height while you're cooking, washing dishes, or working at a kitchen island. About 125,200 units have been recalled.

The hazard: the stools can collapse or tip over while in use, and more seriously, a child's torso can fit through the openings on the front and back sides — creating an entrapment risk that CPSC classifies as a risk of death. There have been 25 reported incidents and 8 confirmed injuries, ranging from scrapes and bruises to a fractured arm.

Here's what to do right now:
— Stop using the stool immediately
— Check your stool against the recalled models: CS0003 and CS0092-4 (sold on Amazon April 2021–November 2025, ~$70 each)
— Call Cosyland at 866-677-3889, Monday–Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM PT, or email customerservice@cosyland.com
— Cosyland will mail you a free repair kit (protective nets, stabilizing feet, and installation guide)
— More information at cpsc.gov/Recalls

If you're not sure whether your stool is affected, check the model number on the product label and compare it against the recall page. When in doubt, don't let kids use it until you've confirmed.

Tagging this for visibility — share with any parents, grandparents, or caregivers in your circle.

---

article.headline: CPSC Recall: 125,000 Cosyland Children's Tower Stools Recalled Over Entrapment and Fall Hazard

stories.title: Fall Youth Sports Registration Is Open — Football Weigh-Ins Start Today

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
COS fall sports registration is open — and football weigh-ins start TODAY at the Sports Office.

Soccer: $86 | Tackle Football: $126
Registration closes July 11.

Register at ColoradoSprings.gov/YouthSports

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 10:00 AM MT

If you've been meaning to sign your kids up for fall sports in Colorado Springs, here's your reminder: registration is already open — and time is moving.

Colorado Springs Parks, Recreation, and Cultural Services opened fall youth sports registration on May 12. Both youth soccer and tackle football are available, with registration closing July 11. Late registration opens July 17, but it comes with a $5 fee per participant.

Here's the quick breakdown:

Youth Soccer — $86/participant
Divisions: pre-K and kindergarten, 1st and 2nd grade, and 7th–9th grade (all coed)

Tackle Football — $126/participant
Divisions: 1st/2nd Coed, 3rd/4th Coed, 5th/6th Boys and Girls, 7th/8th Boys and Girls

One important thing if your kids want to play tackle football: weigh-ins are required, and they start TODAY, May 26, at the Sports Office. Weigh-ins run through July 25, so you have time — but it's worth getting on the schedule now since spaces in some divisions are limited.

Register online at ColoradoSprings.gov/YouthSports. If you have questions, the Sports Office can help.

Does your family have a fall sports tradition in COS? Tell us in the comments.

---

stories.title: D49 Free Summer Meals Start June 1 — All Kids Ages 1 to 18 Welcome

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
COS families: D49 offers FREE breakfast and lunch to all kids ages 1–18 starting June 1.

No income requirements. No D49 enrollment needed. Runs June 1–19.

Spread the word to every family who needs it.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 12:00 PM MT

Here's some genuinely helpful news for Colorado Springs families heading into the summer.

Starting Monday, June 1, District 49 will provide free breakfast and lunch to any child ages 1 to 18 — no income verification, no D49 enrollment required. The program runs through June 19.

That means if you live in the eastern Colorado Springs or Falcon area, your kids can get two free meals a day for nearly three weeks starting next Monday, regardless of which school or district they attend (or whether they're in school at all).

Summer meal programs like this one exist specifically because food access for many families changes when school is out and the regular breakfast/lunch programs stop. D49 has run this program in previous years and it's one of those quiet community resources that makes a real difference for families who use it.

If you know of a family who could use this — a neighbor, a family at your church, a friend with young kiddos — share this post. Details can also be found on the D49 website at d49.org.

What's a resource in COS that you think more families should know about?

---

stories.title: D11 Graduation Week Wraps Up — Adult Ed Ceremony May 28

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
D11 graduation week is wrapping up in Colorado Springs.

High schools held ceremonies May 19–21 at Ed Robson Arena.

One more: Adult and Family Education Graduation — Thursday, May 28, 7 PM, Roy J. Wasson Campus.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 3:00 PM MT

COS, graduation season is almost wrapped.

D11 held its high school ceremonies from May 19–21 at Ed Robson Arena at Colorado College — Palmer, Mitchell, Coronado, Doherty, and several more schools celebrated their class of 2026 over those three days.

But there's one more ceremony worth noting: the D11 Adult and Family Education Graduation, happening this Thursday, May 28, at 7 PM at the Roy J. Wasson Academic Campus.

This one's for the adults in our community — parents, grandparents, working people — who went back to earn their diploma or equivalency credential while managing everything else life throws at you. It's the kind of graduation that often goes unannounced and under-attended, and it deserves a moment.

If you know someone graduating from the Adult and Family Education program this Thursday, go cheer them on. And if you missed one of the high school ceremonies earlier this week — congratulations to every grad in your household.

To every COS family who made it through another school year: we're proud of you, too.

Who in your life is graduating this year?

---

stories.title: Accessible Adventures Are Back — COS Expands Trackchair Program for 2026

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Colorado Springs: the Trackchair program is back with 2 guided hikes daily, Wed–Sun.

Ute Valley Park, Red Rock Canyon, Blodgett + Bluestem Prairie Open Space.

8–11 AM or 12–3 PM. Accessible for all ability levels.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 5:00 PM MT

Colorado Springs has one of the coolest outdoor programs in the state — and if you haven't heard of the Trackchair program, now's the time.

The city's Parks, Recreation, and Cultural Services expanded the program for 2026, offering two guided hikes per day, Wednesday through Sunday, at four of Colorado Springs' most beautiful open spaces:

Ute Valley Park
Red Rock Canyon Open Space
Blodgett Open Space
Bluestem Prairie Open Space

Trackchairs are motorized all-terrain wheelchairs designed for outdoor trails — they can handle the uneven terrain that's kept some parks out of reach for people with mobility challenges. Whether you have a family member who uses a wheelchair, a grandparent who's had trouble with uneven ground, or just wants an experience that works for everyone in the group, this program is worth knowing about.

Hike windows: 8–11 AM and 12–3 PM, weather permitting.

Reservations are required — details and booking at coloradosprings.gov/parks.

Have you tried the Trackchair program before? Tell us which park was your favorite.

---
